---
title: "Social Preference: Multilevel Meta-analysis"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: false
    theme: flatly
date: "2025-12-14"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  results = "asis",
  fig.align = "center"
)
options(width = 120)
```

# Purpose and scope

This report presents a multilevel meta-analysis of social preference across experimental categories.
For each category, effects are synthesised using a multilevel REML model with random effects at the study and experiment levels.

---

```{r full-analysis-pipeline, include=FALSE}
############################
# PACKAGES
############################

pkgs <- c("tidyverse","metafor","clubSandwich","orchaRd","patchwork")

for(p in pkgs){
  if(!requireNamespace(p, quietly = TRUE)){
    install.packages(p, dependencies = TRUE)
  }
}
invisible(lapply(pkgs, library, character.only = TRUE))

############################
# 1) PATHS & PARAMETERS
############################

DATA_PATH <- "all_social_pref.csv"
OUT_DIR   <- "outputs_social_pref"

dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(OUT_DIR,"figs_png"), showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(OUT_DIR,"tables"), showWarnings = FALSE)

RHO0     <- 0.5
RHO_GRID <- c(0,0.2,0.4,0.5,0.6,0.8)

COL_POINT  <- "#CAB2D6"
COL_LINE   <- "#9C85BF"
COL_BORDER <- "black"

############################
# READ DATA
############################

dat_raw <- read_csv(DATA_PATH, show_col_types = FALSE)
categories <- unique(dat_raw$category)

############################
# FUNCTIONS
############################

prepare_category <- function(dat, cat){

  d <- dat %>%
    filter(category == cat) %>%
    mutate(
      sd_int  = abs(sd_int),
      sd_ctrl = abs(sd_ctrl)
    )

  es <- escalc(
    measure = "SMD",
    m1i = mean_int, sd1i = sd_int, n1i = n_int,
    m2i = mean_ctrl, sd2i = sd_ctrl, n2i = n_ctrl,
    data = d
  )

  if("effect_direction" %in% names(d)){
    flip <- ifelse(tolower(d$effect_direction) == "indirect", -1, 1)
    es$yi <- es$yi * flip
  }

  es %>%
    mutate(
      study_id = factor(study_id),
      exp_id   = factor(exp_id),
      effect_id = factor(effect_id),
      SE = sqrt(vi),
      SE_eff = sqrt(1/n_int + 1/n_ctrl),
      study_label = gsub("_"," ", study_id),
      intervention = name_int
    )
}

make_V <- function(es, rho){
  vcalc(
    vi      = es$vi,
    cluster = es$exp_id,
    obs     = es$effect_id,
    rho     = rho
  )
}

############################
# STORAGE OBJECTS
############################

orchard_list   <- list()
rho_sensitivity <- list()

############################
# MAIN LOOP
############################

for(cat in categories){

  message("Running category: ", cat)

  es <- prepare_category(dat_raw, cat)
  V  <- make_V(es, RHO0)

  ############################
  # MAIN MULTILEVEL MODEL
  ############################

  mod <- rma.mv(
    yi = yi,
    V  = V,
    random = ~ 1 | study_id/exp_id,
    method = "REML",
    data = es
  )

  mod_r <- robust(mod, cluster = es$study_id)

  i2_res <- i2_ml(mod_r)

############################
# MAIN RESULTS TABLE
############################

  main_tbl <- tibble(
    category = cat,
    estimate = as.numeric(coef(mod_r)),
    ci_lb = mod_r$ci.lb,
    ci_ub = mod_r$ci.ub,
    p_value = summary(mod_r)$pval,
    sigma2_between = mod_r$sigma2[1],
    sigma2_within  = mod_r$sigma2[2],
    k = mod_r$k,
    n_studies = n_distinct(es$study_id)
  )

  write_csv(main_tbl,
            file.path(OUT_DIR,"tables",paste0("main_meta_",cat,".csv")))

  i2_tbl <- tibble(
    category = cat,
    I2_total   = as.numeric(i2_res["I2_Total"]),
    I2_between = as.numeric(i2_res["I2_study_id"]),
    I2_within  = as.numeric(i2_res["I2_study_id/exp_id"])
  )

  write_csv(
    i2_tbl,
    file.path(OUT_DIR, "tables", paste0("i2_multilevel_", cat, ".csv"))
  )

############################
# FOREST PLOT
############################

  n_es <- mod_r$k
fig_height <- max(4000, n_es * 100)

png(
  filename = file.path(OUT_DIR, "figs_png", paste0("forest_", cat, ".png")),
  width = 6000,
  height = fig_height,
  res = 400
)

  forest(
    mod_r,
    slab = es$study_label,
    ilab = cbind(es$intervention),
    ilab.xpos = c(-10),
    xlim = c(-22, 13),
    alim = c(-6, 6),
    refline = 0,
    pch = 22,
    bg = COL_POINT,
    col = COL_LINE,
    border = COL_BORDER,
    colout = "black",
    addpred = TRUE,
    predstyle = "dist"
  )

  text(-10, mod_r$k + 2, "Intervention", font = 2)
  dev.off()

############################
# ORCHARD PLOT
############################

  op <- orchard_plot(
    mod_r,
    group = "study_id",
    xlab = "Standardised Mean Difference (SMD)",
    legend.pos = "none") + 
    scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Overall effect")) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = COL_POINT) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = COL_LINE) +
    ggtitle(cat)

  ggsave(
    file.path(OUT_DIR,"figs_png",paste0("orchard_",cat,".png")),
    op,
    width = 7,
    height = 3,
    dpi = 400
  )

  orchard_list[[cat]] <- op

############################
# EGGER REGRESSIONS
############################

  egger_SE <- rma.mv(
    yi=yi, V=V,
    random=~1|study_id/exp_id,
    mods=~SE, test="t", data=es
  )

  egger_SEeff <- rma.mv(
    yi=yi, V=V,
    random=~1|study_id/exp_id,
    mods=~SE_eff, test="t", data=es
  )
  
ci_SE     <- confint(egger_SE)
ci_SEeff <- confint(egger_SEeff)

  egger_tbl <- tibble(
    category = cat,
    slope_SE = coef(egger_SE)[2],
    p_SE = summary(egger_SE)$pval[2],
    slope_SEeff = coef(egger_SEeff)[2],
    p_SEeff = summary(egger_SEeff)$pval[2]
  )

  write_csv(egger_tbl,
            file.path(OUT_DIR,"tables",paste0("egger_",cat,".csv")))

############################
# FUNNEL PLOT
############################

 png(file.path(OUT_DIR,"figs_png",paste0("funnel_",cat,".png")),
      width=3000,height=2500,res=400)

  funnel(mod_r,
         xlab="Effect size (SMD)",
         ylab="Sampling Error",
         refline= coef(mod_r)[1])
  dev.off()

  es <- es %>%
    mutate(
      n_total = n_int + n_ctrl,
      precision_n = 1 / sqrt(n_total)
    )

  fp_n <- ggplot(es, aes(x = yi, y = precision_n)) +
    geom_point(
      shape = 21,
      fill = "white",
      colour = "black",
      size = 2
    ) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = coef(mod_r)[1], linetype = "dashed") +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(
      x = "Effect size (SMD)",
      y = "Precision (1/√n)"
    )

  ggsave(
    file.path(OUT_DIR, "figs_png", paste0("funnel_n_", cat, ".png")),
    fp_n,
    width = 6,
    height = 5,
    dpi = 400
  )

############################
# BUBBLE PLOT FOR SMALL STUDY EFFECTS
############################ 

  bp <- orchaRd::bubble_plot(
    egger_SE,
    mod = "SE",
    group = "study_id",   
    xlab  = "Sampling error (SE)",
    ylab  = "Effect size estimates (SMD)",
    legend.pos = "none"
  )

  ggsave(file.path(OUT_DIR,"figs_png",paste0("bubble_",cat,".png")),
         bp,width=7,height=5,dpi=400)  

############################
# LEAVE-ONE-STUDY-OUT
############################

  loso_tbl <- map_dfr(unique(es$study_id), function(st){

    es_loso <- es %>% filter(study_id != st)
    V_loso  <- make_V(es_loso, RHO0)

    m_loso <- rma.mv(
      yi = yi,
      V  = V_loso,
      random = ~ 1 | study_id/exp_id,
      method = "REML",
      data = es_loso
    )

    ci_l <- confint(m_loso)

    tibble(
      category = cat,
      left_out = as.character(st),
      estimate = as.numeric(coef(m_loso)),
      ci_lb = m_loso$ci.lb,
      ci_ub = m_loso$ci.ub,
      p_value = summary(m_loso)$pval,
      sigma2_between = m_loso$sigma2[1],
      sigma2_within  = m_loso$sigma2[2],
      k = m_loso$k,
      n_studies = n_distinct(es_loso$study_id)
    )
  })

  write_csv(
    loso_tbl,
    file.path(OUT_DIR,"tables",paste0("leave_one_out_full_",cat,".csv"))
  )

############################
# EXCLUDE HIGH RISK OF BIAS
############################

  es_low <- es %>% filter(overall_rob != "High")
  V_low  <- make_V(es_low, RHO0)

  m_low <- rma.mv(
    yi = yi,
    V  = V_low,
    random = ~ 1 | study_id/exp_id,
    method = "REML",
    data = es_low
  )

  rob_tbl <- tibble(
   category = cat,
    estimate = as.numeric(coef(m_low)),
    ci_lb = m_low$ci.lb,
    ci_ub = m_low$ci.ub,
    p_value = summary(m_low)$pval,
    sigma2_between = m_low$sigma2[1],
    sigma2_within  = m_low$sigma2[2],
    k = m_low$k,
    n_studies = n_distinct(es_low$study_id)
  )

  write_csv(
    rob_tbl,
    file.path(OUT_DIR,"tables",paste0("risk_bias_full_",cat,".csv"))
  )

############################
# RHO SENSITIVITY
############################

  rho_cat <- map_dfr(RHO_GRID, function(r){

    Vr <- make_V(es, r)

    mr <- rma.mv(
      yi = yi,
      V  = Vr,
      random = ~ 1 | study_id/exp_id,
      method = "REML",
      data = es
    )

    tibble(
       category = cat,
      rho = r,
      estimate = as.numeric(coef(mr)),
      ci_lb = mr$ci.lb,
      ci_ub = mr$ci.ub,
      p_value = summary(mr)$pval,
      sigma2_between = mr$sigma2[1],
      sigma2_within  = mr$sigma2[2],
      k = mr$k,
      n_studies = n_distinct(es$study_id)
    )
  })

  rho_sensitivity[[cat]] <- rho_cat

  rho_all <- bind_rows(rho_sensitivity)
  write_csv(
    rho_all,
    file.path(OUT_DIR,"tables","rho_sensitivity_all_categories_full.csv")
  )
}

############################
# FOREST MISCELLANEOUS
############################

dat_misc <- read_csv("miscellaneous_social_pref.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

es_misc <- dat_misc %>%
  mutate(
    sd_int  = abs(sd_int),
    sd_ctrl = abs(sd_ctrl)
  )

es_misc <- escalc(
  measure = "SMD",
  m1i = mean_int, sd1i = sd_int, n1i = n_int,
  m2i = mean_ctrl, sd2i = sd_ctrl, n2i = n_ctrl,
  data = es_misc
)

if ("effect_direction" %in% names(es_misc)) {
  flip <- ifelse(tolower(es_misc$effect_direction) == "indirect", -1, 1)
  es_misc$yi <- es_misc$yi * flip
}

es_misc <- es_misc %>%
  mutate(
    study_label = gsub("_", " ", study_id),
    intervention = name_int
  )

mod_fake <- rma.uni(
  yi = es_misc$yi,
  vi = es_misc$vi,
  method = "FE"
)

n_es_misc <- mod_fake$k
fig_height_misc <- max(4000, n_es_misc * 100)

png(
  file.path(OUT_DIR, "figs_png", "forest_miscellaneous.png"),
  width = 8000,
  height = fig_height_misc,
  res = 400
)

forest(
  mod_fake,
  slab = es_misc$study_label,
  ilab = cbind(es_misc$intervention, es_misc$pharmacological_action),
  ilab.xpos = c(-52, -29),
  xlim = c(-72, 25),
  alim = c(-10, 10),
  refline = 0,
  addfit = FALSE,
  addpred = FALSE,
  pch = 22,
  bg  = COL_POINT,
  col = COL_LINE,
  border = COL_BORDER
)

text(-52, mod_fake$k + 2, "Intervention", font = 2)
text(-29, mod_fake$k + 2, "Pharmacological action", font = 2)

dev.off()

############################
# META-REGRESSION ETHANOL CONCENTRATION
############################
dat_conc <- read_csv("ethanol_social_pref.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

dat_conc <- dat_conc %>%
mutate(
sd_int  = abs(sd_int),
sd_ctrl = abs(sd_ctrl),
concentration = as.numeric(concentration)
) %>%
filter(!is.na(concentration))

es_conc <- escalc(
measure = "SMD",
m1i = mean_int, sd1i = sd_int, n1i = n_int,
m2i = mean_ctrl, sd2i = sd_ctrl, n2i = n_ctrl,
data = dat_conc
)

if ("effect_direction" %in% names(dat_conc)) {
flip <- ifelse(tolower(dat_conc$effect_direction) == "indirect", -1, 1)
es_conc$yi <- es_conc$yi * flip
}

es_conc <- es_conc %>%
mutate(
study_id  = factor(study_id),
exp_id    = factor(exp_id),
effect_id = factor(effect_id)
)

V_conc <- vcalc(
vi      = es_conc$vi,
cluster = es_conc$exp_id,
obs     = es_conc$effect_id,
rho     = RHO0
)

mod_conc <- rma.mv(
yi = yi,
V  = V_conc,
random = ~ 1 | study_id/exp_id,
mods   = ~ concentration,
test   = "t",
method = "REML",
data   = es_conc
)

mod_conc_r <- robust(mod_conc, cluster = es_conc$study_id)

write_csv(
broom::tidy(mod_conc_r, conf.int = TRUE),
file.path(OUT_DIR, "tables", "meta_regression_concentration.csv")
)

p_conc <- bubble_plot(
mod_conc_r,
mod   = "concentration",
group = "study_id",
xlab  = "Concentration (%)",
ylab  = "Standardised Mean Difference (SMD)",
legend.pos = "none"
)

ggsave(
file.path(OUT_DIR, "figs_png", "bubble_meta_regression_concentration.png"),
p_conc,
width = 7,
height = 5,
dpi = 400
)

############################
# COMBINED ORCHARD PLOT
############################

combined_orchard <- wrap_plots(
  orchard_list,
  ncol = 2
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(OUT_DIR, "figs_png", "orchard_all_categories.png"),
  plot = combined_orchard,
  width = 14,
  height = 3 * ceiling(length(orchard_list) / 2),
  dpi = 400
)
```

# Results by category

```{r narrative-results}
for(cat in categories){

  cat("\n\n## Category: ", cat, "\n", sep = "")

  es <- prepare_category(dat_raw, cat)
  V  <- make_V(es, RHO0)
  mod <- rma.mv(
    yi = yi,
    V  = V,
    random = ~ 1 | study_id/exp_id,
    method = "REML",
    data = es
  )
  
  mod_r <- robust(mod, cluster = es$study_id)

  cat("\n### Overall effect\n\n```text\n")
  print(mod_r)
  cat("```\n")

  main_tbl <- read_csv(
    file.path(OUT_DIR,"tables",paste0("main_meta_",cat,".csv")),
    show_col_types = FALSE
  )

  print(knitr::kable(main_tbl, digits = 4))

  cat("\n### Forest plot\n")
  cat("\n**Caption.** Forest plot of individual effect sizes and the pooled multilevel estimate, including a prediction interval.\n")
  cat("**Interpretation.** The pooled summary represents the average effect; the prediction interval reflects the expected range of true effects in new studies given heterogeneity.\n")
  cat(paste0("\n![](", file.path(OUT_DIR,"figs_png",paste0("forest_",cat,".png")), ")\n"))

  cat("\n### Heterogeneity and variance decomposition\n")
  i2_tbl <- read_csv(
    file.path(OUT_DIR,"tables",paste0("i2_multilevel_",cat,".csv")),
    show_col_types = FALSE
  )
  print(knitr::kable(i2_tbl, digits = 3))
  cat("\n**Interpretation.** I² quantifies the proportion of variability attributable to heterogeneity rather than sampling error, decomposed into between- and within-study components.\n")

  cat("\n### Distribution of effects (orchard plot)\n")
  cat("\n**Caption.** Orchard plot for the multilevel model for this category (PNG saved by the pipeline; colour scheme matches the original script).\n")
  cat("**Interpretation.** Orchard plots complement forest plots by visualising the distribution of effects with multilevel uncertainty and heterogeneity.\n")
  cat(paste0("\n![](", file.path(OUT_DIR,"figs_png",paste0("orchard_",cat,".png")), ")\n"))

  cat("\n### Small-study effects and publication bias\n")

  egger_tbl <- read_csv(
    file.path(OUT_DIR,"tables",paste0("egger_",cat,".csv")),
    show_col_types = FALSE
  )
  cat("\n#### Egger-type regressions\n")
  print(knitr::kable(egger_tbl, digits = 4))
  cat("\n**Interpretation.** The slope tests whether effect sizes are associated with sampling error (SE) or effective SE. Consistent significance may indicate small-study effects.\n")

  cat("\n#### Funnel plot (Sampling Error)\n")
  cat("\n**Caption.** Funnel plot using Sampling Error.\n")
  cat("**Interpretation.** Symmetry suggests little evidence of small-study effects; asymmetry suggests potential dissemination bias or related mechanisms.\n")
  cat(paste0("\n![](", file.path(OUT_DIR,"figs_png",paste0("funnel_",cat,".png")), ")\n"))

  cat("\n#### Funnel plot (precision = 1/√n)\n")
  cat("\n**Caption.** Funnel plot using 1/√n as an alternative measure of precision.\n")
  cat("**Interpretation.** Agreement between the two funnel diagnostics strengthens inference about small-study effects.\n")
  cat(paste0("\n![](", file.path(OUT_DIR,"figs_png",paste0("funnel_n_",cat,".png")), ")\n"))

  cat("\n#### Bubble plot (Egger regression)\n")
  cat("\n**Caption.** Bubble plot of effect sizes versus sampling error (SE), with study clustering.\n")
  cat("**Interpretation.** A visually non-zero slope is consistent with Egger regression evidence for small-study effects; a near-zero slope supports their absence.\n")
  cat(paste0("\n![](", file.path(OUT_DIR,"figs_png",paste0("bubble_",cat,".png")), ")\n"))

  cat("\n### Sensitivity analyses\n")

  cat("\n#### 5.1 Leave-one-study-out\n")
  cat("This analysis repeats the meta-analysis while removing one study at a time to assess influence.\n")
  cat("**Interpretation.** Large shifts in the pooled estimate indicate influential studies; stability indicates robustness.\n")

  loso_tbl <- read_csv(
    file.path(OUT_DIR,"tables",paste0("leave_one_out_full_",cat,".csv")),
    show_col_types = FALSE
  )
  print(knitr::kable(loso_tbl, digits = 4))

  cat("\n#### 5.2 Excluding high risk-of-bias studies\n")
  cat("This analysis repeats the meta-analysis after excluding studies classified as high risk of bias.\n")
  cat("**Interpretation.** Differences from the main pooled effect indicate sensitivity to study quality; similar results suggest robustness.\n")

  rob_tbl <- read_csv(
    file.path(OUT_DIR,"tables",paste0("risk_bias_full_",cat,".csv")),
    show_col_types = FALSE
  )
  print(knitr::kable(rob_tbl, digits = 4))

  cat("\n#### Sensitivity to within-study correlation (rho)\n")
  cat("This analysis varies the assumed within-study correlation among effect sizes.\n")
  cat("**Interpretation.** Stability of pooled estimates across rho values indicates robustness to assumptions about within-study dependence.\n")

  rho_tbl <- read_csv(
    file.path(OUT_DIR,"tables","rho_sensitivity_all_categories_full.csv"),
    show_col_types = FALSE
  ) %>% dplyr::filter(category == cat)

  print(knitr::kable(rho_tbl, digits = 4))
}
```

# Combined orchard plots across all categories

```{r combined-orchard}
wrap_plots(orchard_list, ncol = 2)
```

**Caption.** Combined orchard plots for all categories.  
**Interpretation.** This panel supports direct comparison of pooled effects and uncertainty across categories.

# Miscellaneous studies

```{r misc-forest}
knitr::include_graphics(
  file.path(OUT_DIR,"figs_png","forest_miscellaneous.png")
)
```

**Caption.** Forest plot for miscellaneous studies (descriptive fixed-effect display).  
**Interpretation.** Miscellaneous studies are shown descriptively and not pooled into the main multilevel models due to conceptual heterogeneity.

# Meta-regression by ethanol concentration
Multilevel meta-regression examining whether the standardised mean difference (SMD) varies as a function of ethanol concentration (%).
The analysis was conducted using a multilevel REML model with random effects at the study and experiment levels.

```{r meta-regression-output, results="asis"}
cat("```text\n")
print(summary(mod_conc_r))
cat("```\n")
```

```{r bubb-ethanol}
knitr::include_graphics(
file.path(OUT_DIR, "figs_png", "bubble_meta_regression_concentration.png")
)
```
**Caption.** Bubble plot of effect sizes as a function of ethanol concentration (%). Each point represents an effect size, with clustering by study.
**Interpretation.** The slope of the meta-regression represents the expected change in the standardised mean difference per one percentage point increase in ethanol concentration. A slope close to zero indicates little evidence for a dose–response relationship, whereas a non-zero slope suggests that ethanol concentration moderates the magnitude of the social preference effect.

# Computational environment

```{r session-info}
cat("R version: ", R.version.string, "\n")
cat("Platform: ", paste(Sys.info()[c("sysname","release","machine")], collapse = " / "), "\n")
cat("Render date: 2025-12-14\n\n")
sessionInfo()
```
